design_philosophy.core_loop: Select protagonist career path → manage fleet and resources → engage in trading, privateering, treasure hunting, and exploration → advance character career and plot through open-ended sandbox with story gates
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Career progression gating vs open-ended exploration freedom; balancing commercial success with story advancement requirements
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Age of Discovery captain commanding a seagoing fleet across the globe, establishing trading networks, discovering new lands, hunting treasure, and building personal glory and family legacy
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game emphasizes player choice through six distinct protagonist career paths (explorer, pirate, privateer, cartographer, treasure hunter, merchant) with different mechanical focuses and narrative objectives. Loose plot structure allows deviation but progression requires following certain story paths—a hybrid of sandbox freedom and narrative structure. Historical Age of Discovery setting idealized to prioritize adventure over colonial complexity.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player controls career direction, fleet management, trading strategies, and exploration pace. Different protagonists likely have varying mechanical difficulty baselines (e.g., pirate vs merchant paths may have different economic/combat emphasis)

progression.upgrade_system: Fleet expansion and management; ship upgrades implied through trading and treasure hunting mechanics; career advancement through completing objectives tied to protagonist choice
progression.unlock_mechanism: Career development required to advance plot; different protagonists unlock different story branches and mechanical focus areas (João Franco pursues Atlantis discovery, Catalina pursues revenge against Franco family, Otto undertakes secret anti-Spanish missions, etc.)
replayability_hooks: Six distinct protagonist paths with different career focuses and narratives
Open-ended sandbox structure allowing multiple approaches to advancement
Multiple countries and neutral ports to engage with commercially
Exploration-driven discovery mechanics rewarding new port discovery

world_building_techniques: Historical Age of Discovery setting (15th-17th century European exploration)
Multiple protagonists with distinct personal narratives and motivations
Loose plot structure tied to character career paths rather than rigid quest chains
Environmental world building through port discovery and global exploration
References to historical events (Treaty of Tordesillas mentioned) and rivalries (Spain vs Portugal)
FMV opening cutscenes in later console ports (Saturn, PlayStation versions)

faction_indicators: Six national/cultural factions: Portugal, Spain, Ottoman Empire, England, Dutch, Italian
Career path choice signals player allegiance (explorer, pirate, privateer, cartographer, treasure hunter, merchant)
Historical rivalries: Spain vs Portugal, Spanish Armada opposition (Otto's path)
danger_signals: Privateer path implies state-sanctioned naval conflict
Pirate protagonist (Catalina) suggests moral ambiguity and revenge motivations
Treasure hunting and debt accumulation mechanics imply resource scarcity pressure
environmental_storytelling: Port investment mechanics showing sphere of influence expansion
Fleet composition and ship choices reflecting career path (combat-focused vs trade-focused)
Global exploration revealing Age of Discovery geography and trading networks
